reviewer: agent matrix pass (runner-evidenced + Node runnability proof, revision 2)

rubric.evidence: conformance (webgpu, headless no-GPU): 2✓ 0✗ 0▨ 0◍ — loads-clean=pass, calls-real-inference=pass. screenshots: reports/responsive/screens/outetts/desktop.png, reports/responsive/screens/outetts/mobile.png

Node transformers.js runnability proof (the official outetts library, exactly per the model card): generate({text:"Hello from the browser."}) returned {audio: Tensor, sr: 24000} → 39040 Float32 samples (~1.63 s), peak amplitude 0.67, meanAbs 0.045 → NON-SILENT real speech. audioTokens ≈ durSec*75 (WavTokenizer rate). In-browser generation on the WASM path was not completable on the memory-constrained headless runner (needs WebGPU/more RAM).
rubric.notes: Output shape (24 kHz waveform) + non-silence confirmed in Node; full in-browser generation needs a capable device (see support/openQuestions).

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Keep the heavy autoregressive LLM + codec decode off the main thread so interaction latency (INP) stays low.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — all synthesis (Qwen2 LLM token generation + WavTokenizer decode) runs in a Web Worker (models/outetts/worker.js); the main thread only draws the waveform and updates the readout.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: conformance inference-off-main-thread=pass; new Worker in outetts-ui.js

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive control panel without horizontal overflow
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use grid repeat(auto-fit, minmax()) for the controls, flex-wrap for chip/audio rows, min-inline-size:0 on flex items, and range inputs that flex to fit.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — .controls-grid is grid auto-fit minmax(220px,1fr); .audio-row / .chip-row / .pipe wrap; range inputs use min-inline-size:0. Live scan overflow=0 on desktop (1280) and mobile (360).

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Wide media (waveform SVG, pipeline diagram) must fit/scroll within its container, never spill the page.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — .wave-svg is inline-size:100% max-inline-size:100% block-size:auto; the pipeline is a flex-wrap row. clipped=0 both classes.

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce load/generation status in a role=status aria-live region; label every control; the audio element uses native controls; the waveform SVG carries role=img + aria-label.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — createModelLoader status is role=status aria-live; each page has a role=status region; sliders/selects are labelled; waveform SVG has aria-label. buttons-have-names=pass, live-status-region=pass, images-have-alt=pass.

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use cross-document view transitions for catalogue↔page navigation.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — @view-transition navigation:auto in the shared public/styles.css (invariant 6).

openQuestions: The ~580 MB q8 model on the WASM path exceeds the memory-constrained headless runner (renderer unresponsive through 6+ min); it needs confirming on a real device with WebGPU or more RAM. Runnability itself is proven in Node (non-silent 24 kHz audio).
Should the demo offer a lighter dtype (q4/q4f16) toggle to reduce the memory footprint on constrained devices?
Are all sliders/selects keyboard-operable with visible focus on both classes (spot-checked, not exhaustively driven)?

followUpGoals.goal: Confirm OuteTTS end-to-end generation in a real browser on a WebGPU-capable device (or higher-RAM runner): Read the played waveform + token/RTF readout, then flip support to ok. Same posture as kokoro-text-to-speech.
Add an optional q4/q4f16 dtype toggle so constrained devices can run the LLM in less memory; record the quality/latency trade-off.

summary: Revision-2 critique for outetts (onnx-community/OuteTTS-0.2-500M, text-to-speech, LLM+codec). DISTINCT from Kokoro/SpeechT5/MMS-TTS: an LLM that generates audio tokens decoded by the WavTokenizer codec. Runnability PROVEN in Node transformers.js (39040 non-silent 24 kHz samples, peak 0.67, for a real phrase; the official outetts library, exactly per the model card). Live headless-Chrome run confirmed the honest first-visit Download (~580 MB) state and clean responsive layout on both classes (screenshots read); full in-browser generation is memory/compute-heavy and was not completable on the WASM-only headless runner (needs WebGPU/more RAM) — recorded honestly as needs-review, matching the sibling TTS. Conformance: 15/15 auto-tested pass, 0 fail/blocked, 8 manual. Guidance ids identify-inp-causes, css-layout, overflow-clipping-control, accessibility, cross-document-transitions applied.
